title_en: A no-brainer for protecting your brain
rubric_en: One simple vaccination may dramatically reduce the risk of dementia
rubric_zh: 一种简单的疫苗接种或许能大幅降低痴呆风险

paragraphs.en: FEW CONDITIONS are as feared as dementia, of which Alzheimer’s is the most common cause. This is mostly because of its insidious nature, since it strips people of their sense of self and leaves those who love them caring for a stranger. “Alzheimer’s is me unwinding, losing trust in myself, a butt of my own jokes and, on bad days, capable of playing hunt the slipper by myself, and losing,” wrote the late Sir Terry Pratchett, a novelist who had a rare form of it. “I felt totally alone, with the world receding from me in every direction.”
paragraphs.zh: 很少有疾病像痴呆症那样令人恐惧，而阿尔茨海默病是其中最常见的原因。这种恐惧很大程度上来自它的隐匿性，因为它会剥夺一个人的自我意识，让那些深爱他的人最终照顾一个陌生人。已故小说家特里·普拉切特曾写道：“阿尔茨海默病让我不断解体，失去对自己的信任，成了自己玩笑的对象，在最糟糕的时候，甚至会自己在‘捉迷藏’里迷失。”他还说：“我感到完全孤独，世界正从四面八方从我身边退开。”

paragraphs.en: It is also dreaded because many believe it is becoming far more common and so will inevitably strike them or those closest to them. Some epidemiologists in the field seem only to fan those fears. Models predict that the number of people suffering from dementia may triple to 153m by 2050 as societies age. In truth, the outlook is cheerier than that, especially in rich countries where, if adjusted for age, the risk of getting dementia has dropped sharply in recent decades. Most models assume these declines will not continue, or will even reverse. A mountain of new evidence shows that need not be.
paragraphs.zh: 它也令人恐惧，因为许多人相信这种疾病正在变得越来越常见，迟早会降临在自己或最亲近的人身上。领域内一些流行病学家似乎只是在不断放大这种担忧。模型预测，随着社会老龄化，到2050年罹患痴呆症的人数可能增长到1.53亿。实际上，前景比想象中更乐观，尤其是在发达国家，按年龄校正后，近几十年来患痴呆症的风险已经显著下降。大多数模型假设这种下降不会持续，甚至可能逆转。但大量新证据表明并非必然如此。

paragraphs.en: Recent studies point out the numerous ways in which people are already reducing their risk of dementia. Many are lifestyle changes that you already know you should be making, such as eating healthily, exercising more and keeping your brain active. Others are medical interventions, such as treating hearing loss, depression, high blood pressure and high cholesterol. But getting people to adopt, and then stick to, healthy habits is hard. It does not help that, ideally, you should be working out more and boozing less for many years before your brain will thank you.
paragraphs.zh: 近期研究指出，人们已经在多方面降低痴呆风险。许多是你早已知道该做的生活方式调整，比如健康饮食、更多运动，以及保持大脑活跃。另一些则是医学干预，比如治疗听力损失、抑郁、高血压和高胆固醇。然而，让人们采纳并坚持这些健康习惯并不容易。更不用说，理想状态下，你通常需要在大脑真正“回报”你之前，就持续多年增加运动量、减少饮酒。

paragraphs.en: Yet there is one direct way to improve your chances of staying mentally sharp, and it involves almost no toil, tears or sweat. One of the most exciting scientific findings in recent years is that a course of the shingles vaccine may reduce the risk of dementia by about 20%. For a simple intervention, that is a huge benefit. Exactly why this happens is still being debated. One theory is that the varicella-zoster virus, which causes both shingles and chickenpox, contributes to dementia by causing damage or inflammation to the nervous system even while it is supposedly dormant. Another is that the vaccination gives the immune system a firm kick up its B-cells, activating it against other bugs that might contribute to dementia.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，还有一种直接提高你保持思维敏锐几率的方法，而且几乎不费力、无泪、无汗。近年来最令人振奋的科学发现之一是，一整程带状疱疹疫苗接种可能将痴呆风险降低约20%。对于一种简单干预来说，这是一个巨大的收益。这种作用机制究竟为何仍在讨论中。一个理论是引起带状疱疹和水痘的水痘带状疱疹病毒，即使处于休眠状态，也会损伤或炎症化神经系统，从而助长痴呆。另一个理论是，疫苗会“踢醒”人体免疫系统中的B细胞，使其对其他可能导致痴呆的病原体保持更强反应。

paragraphs.en: Too many public-health systems fail to offer the vaccine to more than just a small share of those who would benefit. Several studies have shown that, taking into account only the shingles cases prevented, it would be cost-effective to vaccinate almost everyone from about the age of 55. Yet many countries have rationed the vaccine to keep down the upfront costs. Britain, for instance, lowered the eligibility age from 70 to 65 in 2023, but has since been too slow to vaccinate those who were already in their late 60s. Among the 27 members of the European Union, just 17 recommend the vaccine at all, and several reserve it for the over-65s.
paragraphs.zh: 太多公共卫生体系没有向更广泛的受益人群提供这类疫苗，而只覆盖了其中一小部分。多项研究显示，仅就可预防的带状疱疹病例而言，给大约55岁起几乎所有人接种都是具备成本效益的。然而，许多国家为了压低前期支出而对疫苗进行了配给。比如，英国在2023年将接种年龄门槛从70岁下调到65岁，但随后对已经进入60后末期的人群接种推进得太慢。在欧盟27个成员国中，仅有17个国家会推荐接种这款疫苗，而且一些国家只把它限定给65岁以上人群。

paragraphs.en: That makes little sense when considering only its efficacy against shingles, which afflicts 20-30% of unvaccinated people. It makes even less sense given its potential as a weapon in the fight against dementia.
paragraphs.zh: 只从其预防带状疱疹的效果来看，这样做也说不通，因为带状疱疹可侵袭20%到30%的未接种人群。考虑到它在抗击痴呆方面的潜力，事情就更没有道理了。

paragraphs.en: Most of the evidence of its anti-dementia effect relates to an earlier version of the vaccine, which used a weakened form of the live virus. It has since been largely replaced by a new one, Shingrix, which contains just a sprinkling of proteins from the virus and is seen as safer because it cannot cause an infection. Some studies suggest the new jab may be at least as powerful against dementia as the old one. Even so, it would make sense to run randomised trials to learn which is better, the optimal age for getting it and whether boosters are needed.
paragraphs.zh: 有关该疫苗抗痴呆效果的多数证据都来自于早期版本——当时使用的是减毒活病毒。后来它大多被新一代疫苗Shingrix取代，Shingrix只含有少量病毒蛋白片段，因此被认为更安全，因为它不会引发感染。部分研究显示，这款新疫苗对抗痴呆的效果至少与旧疫苗一样强。即便如此，仍然有必要开展随机对照试验，明确哪一种更有效、接种的最佳年龄，以及是否需要追加加强针。

paragraphs.en: In the meantime, health authorities should not wait. A full two-dose course of Shingrix costs Britain’s health service £320 ($430) and federal immunisation programmes in America around $270: a bargain, given the potential savings in long-term care costs. As for individuals, even the retail cost (around £460 in Britain) is a small price to reduce by one-fifth the chances of having the world recede in every direction. ■
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，公共卫生部门不应再等。Shingrix完整的两针接种方案在英国医保体系中的花费为320英镑（约430美元），在美国联邦免疫计划中的费用约为270美元：相比其潜在的长期护理成本节约，这是一笔划算的投入。对于个人来说，即使按英国市场零售价约460英镑来算，也只是为降低人们可能出现认知崩塌、世界感知逐渐与自己远离的概率（约降低五分之一）支付的小成本。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》订阅者可以订阅我们的“观点”通讯，汇集了本刊最优秀的主导文章、专栏、客座文章以及读者来信。
